blocks.text: Barbara Hall, with Jeffrey Ochoa and Megan Dudley copied:

Status on the {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} experience study. The extracts for each valuation date in the observation period are in and reconciled against each other. Headcounts tie except in one year, where roughly sixty records leave the population and reappear in the next extract under different identifiers. That reads as a recordkeeper conversion rather than experience. Left alone it would show up as sixty terminations and sixty new hires in the same year and push the withdrawal rate up for no reason at all.

So the asks are the crosswalk from the old identifiers to the new ones, if the Estes Group recordkeeper kept one, and confirmation of the conversion date. If no crosswalk exists, tell us and we will match those records on date of birth and hire date and disclose that we did it that way. It is a judgment we would rather make in the open than have found in review.

Otherwise we are where we expect to be. Jeffrey Ochoa has the amendment history and has already flagged one change to the early retirement provisions that we will need to hold out separately, and Megan Dudley has the workbook built and waiting on clean exposures. Nothing else needed from you this week. If it is faster to walk your staff through the field definitions on the phone than by mail, say so and I will call them. I wrote the definitions, and I would rather explain them once than reconcile around them twice.
